element_introduction_order.mastery_context: mobility and spacing advantage throughout game
triggers transformation sequences in select characters
dramatic fighting style and appearance changes alter matchup dynamics
high-damage combo finishers and pressure tools
superhuman abilities and mysterious attack patterns

rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Match-based pacing with escalation through tournament rounds; tension rises as opponent AI adapts; transformations create sudden rhythm shifts

difficulty_curve.early_game: Early tournament opponents introduce basic fighting patterns; player learns fundamental mechanics (jump, run, basic attacks)
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Mid-tournament matches require understanding character-specific throw mechanics and transformation triggers; AI becomes more aggressive
difficulty_curve.late_game: Final rounds escalate with opponent special attacks and boss-level patterns; Oume Gōketsuji final boss features superhuman abilities and mysterious attack mechanics
notable_spikes: Boss fight vs. Oume Gōketsuji (final match, unplayable character with palette-swap design)

notable_level_patterns.description: Linear tournament structure where player selects one of 8 characters and fights remaining 7 opponents plus final boss; each character has unique story context (e.g., Otane avenging abuse from Oume, Keith trying to impress Annie Hamilton)
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Character-specific tournament route with 8 total matches

notable_level_patterns.description: Select opponents can execute throwing moves to trigger dramatic appearance and fighting style changes mid-match, creating unpredictable rhythm shifts and requiring player tactical adaptation
SNES-exclusive mode: defeat as many opponents as possible with single life bar; escalating difficulty without tournament story structure
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Endless opponent sequence with cumulative damage pressure

sources_used: Wikipedia - Power Instinct article (game overview, character descriptions, platform ports, gameplay features)
The Cutting Room Floor - Power Instinct (SNES) page (regional differences, SNES-specific modes)
